message:
chore(beads): close bd-qpoys + bd-x0lv6, pick up bd-4uyu5

Routine ledger sync reflecting the FUSE protocol upgrade work
shipped in 8294efe. Three downstream beads of bd-x4l3t shift state:

- bd-qpoys "[FUSE] splice/sendfile pipe-to-file fast path"
  in_progress -> closed
  Reason: "Completed: FUSE splice/sendfile mounted probes pass,
  1GiB sendfile benchmark emits PASS for >=2x throughput, workspace
  check/clippy/fmt clean"

- bd-x0lv6 "[FUSE] inotify watch lifecycle + IN_MOVED_FROM/TO
  atomic rename pairing"
  open -> closed
  Reason: "Completed: FUSE notifier captures kernel notification
  handle for mount paths, invalidates committed unlink/rmdir/rename
  dentries without FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE for plain renames, adds
  mounted inotify rename cookie-pair probes, and updates parity to
  96/96; fmt/check/clippy/UBS clean; mounted probes compile/pass
  via CI soft-skip where FUSE mount permission is denied."

- bd-4uyu5 "[FUSE] RWF_DSYNC + io_uring per-op flags propagation"
  open -> in_progress
  The next downstream of bd-x4l3t to be picked up; routes RWF_*
  flag bits from FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID to OpenFs::ext4_write so
  RWF_DSYNC -> immediate fsync, RWF_NOWAIT -> try_lock on
  FuseInodeLocks, RWF_HIPRI -> nop (no priority queue).

No code changes here; just the .beads/issues.jsonl status flips.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-4ogh","title":"Implement ext4/btrfs parity features: decompression, inline data, bigalloc, ZERO_RANGE","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:28:47.537528441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.634096851Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.633525312Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 new parity features:
(1) btrfs transparent decompression: ZLIB (flate2) + ZSTD in read path for inline and regular extents. Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum. LZO deferred (custom framing).
(2) ext4 inline data read: reads from inode extent_bytes + xattr_ibody when EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL is set. Removed INLINE_DATA from REJECTED_V1 incompat set.
(3) ext4 bigalloc infrastructure: added cluster_ratio to FsGeometry (cluster_size/block_size for BIGALLOC, else 1).
(4) ext4 ZERO_RANGE fallocate: writes zero blocks to existing allocated extents in range. Mode 0x10 now accepted.
All tests pass (471 ffs-core, 360 ffs-ondisk, 142 ffs-alloc, 117 ffs-btrfs, 114 ffs-extent).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ext4","parity"]}

 {"id":"bd-4qb1o","title":"port fscrypt policy-ex ioctl parity in ffs-fuse and ffs-core","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T01:24:01.023080784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:23.330710277Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:23.327706536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-4uyu5","title":"[FUSE] RWF_DSYNC + io_uring per-op flags propagation","description":"Honor pwritev2(2) RWF_* flags (RWF_SYNC, RWF_DSYNC, RWF_HIPRI, RWF_NOWAIT, RWF_APPEND) and io_uring per-SQE flags (IOSQE_FIXED_FILE, IOSQE_IO_LINK, IOSQE_ASYNC) when the kernel forwards them through the FUSE WRITE / IORING_OP_WRITE path.

Context:
- Linux pwritev2 with RWF_DSYNC must commit the write durably (data + minimal metadata) before returning. ext4 implements this via filemap_write_and_wait_range + ext4_sync_file. FrankenFS should map RWF_DSYNC to: stage write -> jbd2 commit_transaction -> dev.sync (data only, not full inode metadata journal).
- RWF_NOWAIT must return EAGAIN rather than block on a contended lock (matches ext4's mapping_check_writeback flag); for ffs-fuse, this means try_lock on FuseInodeLocks instead of blocking acquire.
- io_uring forwards fuse_in_header.flags including FUSE_IOCB_NOWAIT (bit 9) and FUSE_IOCB_DSYNC; the dispatcher needs to read these and route accordingly.

Acceptance:
- A test that issues pwritev2 with RWF_DSYNC, then drops the underlying device dirty cache, then re-mounts -> data is recoverable (no FUA but still durable per the journal commit).
- pwritev2 with RWF_NOWAIT on a parent inode currently held by another mutation returns EAGAIN within 100us, not a multi-ms block.
- xfstests generic/{623,633,634} (RWF_*) pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:56.562180780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:56.562180780Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-4uyu5","title":"[FUSE] RWF_DSYNC + io_uring per-op flags propagation","description":"Honor pwritev2(2) RWF_* flags (RWF_SYNC, RWF_DSYNC, RWF_HIPRI, RWF_NOWAIT, RWF_APPEND) and io_uring per-SQE flags (IOSQE_FIXED_FILE, IOSQE_IO_LINK, IOSQE_ASYNC) when the kernel forwards them through the FUSE WRITE / IORING_OP_WRITE path.

Context:
- Linux pwritev2 with RWF_DSYNC must commit the write durably (data + minimal metadata) before returning. ext4 implements this via filemap_write_and_wait_range + ext4_sync_file. FrankenFS should map RWF_DSYNC to: stage write -> jbd2 commit_transaction -> dev.sync (data only, not full inode metadata journal).
- RWF_NOWAIT must return EAGAIN rather than block on a contended lock (matches ext4's mapping_check_writeback flag); for ffs-fuse, this means try_lock on FuseInodeLocks instead of blocking acquire.
- io_uring forwards fuse_in_header.flags including FUSE_IOCB_NOWAIT (bit 9) and FUSE_IOCB_DSYNC; the dispatcher needs to read these and route accordingly.

Acceptance:
- A test that issues pwritev2 with RWF_DSYNC, then drops the underlying device dirty cache, then re-mounts -> data is recoverable (no FUA but still durable per the journal commit).
- pwritev2 with RWF_NOWAIT on a parent inode currently held by another mutation returns EAGAIN within 100us, not a multi-ms block.
- xfstests generic/{623,633,634} (RWF_*) pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:56.562180780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:37:54.952637798Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-qpoys","title":"[FUSE] splice/sendfile pipe-to-file fast path","description":"Implement zero-copy fast path for splice(2) and sendfile(2) when reading from a pipe and writing to a regular file (or vice-versa) over the FUSE mount.

Context:
- Linux splice/sendfile expect the page cache or pipe-buf to be moved without a userspace bounce. Standard FUSE userspace can't actually bypass userspace, but it CAN advertise FUSE_PASSTHROUGH or use FUSE_NO_OPEN_SUPPORT + dispatcher hints to short-circuit large copies.
- For ffs-fuse, plumbing should: detect splice/sendfile ioctl path (from request flags or FUSE_WRITE / FUSE_READ with the splice flag set), batch the I/O into block-aligned segments, route through MVCC-aware extent mapping without per-byte copy.
- Reference: fs/splice.c, fs/read_write.c::do_sendfile, FUSE FOPEN_DIRECT_IO + FOPEN_NO_FLUSH semantics.

Acceptance:
- A test that writes 16 MiB through splice() (pipe -> file in mounted FUSE) and verifies the data + ensures no per-page memcpy in the hot path that's bigger than O(extents).
- xfstests generic/{255, 263, 462} (splice/sendfile-touching) pass under the FUSE mount with --rw.
- Bench delta: at least 2x throughput vs. the naive read+write loop on a 1 GiB file.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:24:10.085436820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:28:52.952294083Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Context:

- For ffs-fuse, plumbing should: detect splice/sendfile ioctl path (from request flags or FUSE_WRITE / FUSE_READ with the splice flag set), batch the I/O into block-aligned segments, route through MVCC-aware extent mapping without per-byte copy.
- Reference: fs/splice.c, fs/read_write.c::do_sendfile, FUSE FOPEN_DIRECT_IO + FOPEN_NO_FLUSH semantics.

Acceptance:
- A test that writes 16 MiB through splice() (pipe -> file in mounted FUSE) and verifies the data + ensures no per-page memcpy in the hot path that's bigger than O(extents).
- xfstests generic/{255, 263, 462} (splice/sendfile-touching) pass under the FUSE mount with --rw.
- Bench delta: at least 2x throughput vs. the naive read+write loop on a 1 GiB file.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:24:10.085436820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:15:28.546244975Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:15:28.544343554Z","close_reason":"Completed: FUSE splice/sendfile mounted probes pass, 1GiB sendfile benchmark emits PASS for >=2x throughput, workspace check/clippy/fmt clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qpt5","title":"Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation","description":"# Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation

## GOAL
Accelerate CRC32C and BLAKE3 checksum computation using SIMD instructions.

## BACKGROUND (Extreme Software Optimization)
Checksum computation is on the hot path:
- Every block read/write verifies checksum
- ARC cache hits still verify (trust but verify)
- RaptorQ symbols have checksums

SIMD can provide 4-16x speedup depending on instruction set.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. CRC32C Acceleration
```rust
pub fn crc32c_simd(data: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
    #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
    {
        if is_x86_feature_detected!("sse4.2") {
            return crc32c_sse42(data, seed);
        }
    }
    #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]
    {
        if is_aarch64_feature_detected!("crc") {
            return crc32c_arm_crc(data, seed);
        }
    }
    crc32c_fallback(data, seed)
}
```

### 2. BLAKE3 (Already SIMD)
- blake3 crate already uses SIMD
- Ensure proper feature flags enabled
- Benchmark to confirm

### 3. Batch Checksum
```rust
/// Compute checksums for multiple blocks in parallel
pub fn batch_checksum(blocks: &[&[u8]], algo: ChecksumAlgo) -> Vec<u32> {
    // Use rayon for multi-block parallelism
    // Use SIMD within each block
}
```

### 4. Feature Detection at Runtime
- Detect CPU features once at startup
- Store in global (or thread-local)
- Dispatch to appropriate implementation

## TESTS
1. Unit: SIMD matches fallback results
2. Unit: All supported CPUs tested (via CI matrix)
3. Benchmark: Before/after comparison
4. Property: Random data, SIMD == fallback

## LOGGING
- Feature detection (info): detected SIMD capabilities
- Batch checksum (trace): block count, duration

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Correctness: SIMD == fallback for all inputs
2. Performance: >2x speedup on supported CPUs
3. Graceful fallback on unsupported CPUs
4. No unsafe code (use safe SIMD wrappers)

## NOTES
Use existing crates:
- crc32c crate has SIMD support
- blake3 crate is already optimized
Main work is ensuring proper feature flags and benchmarking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:14.096946312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163955333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163936237Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","perf","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.533583752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.930946133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qqowd","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for fsync fdatasync and fsyncdir","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e currently proves fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir success on writable mounts, but the mounted-path read-only rejection contract is not frozen even though ffs-fuse and ffs-core already return EROFS for these sync operations on read-only mounts. Scope: add btrfs read-only mounted-path probes for file sync_all, sync_data, and directory sync_all that assert exact EROFS and unchanged data/dirent state. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only sync refusal for fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:27.269990402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:32:36.746576644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:32:36.744719148Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qrqdk","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] Jbd2Writer emits unchecksummed descriptor/revoke/commit blocks — replay with verify_checksums=true rejects them on checksummed journals","description":"# Review finding (review-only pass on 2026-04-23)

## Location

`crates/ffs-journal/src/lib.rs` `Jbd2Writer::commit_transaction` (lines ~982-1180) and the `Jbd2Writer` struct (~841-851).

## Finding

The JBD2-compatible writer produces three kinds of blocks — descriptor, revoke, and commit — and does not stamp a checksum into any of them:

- Descriptor blocks (L1056-L1095): built with `vec![0; bs]` + header + tags, written verbatim. No tail-checksum at `block[bs-4..bs]`.
- Revoke blocks (L1126-L1153): built with header + entries, written verbatim. No tail-checksum.
- Commit blocks (L1160-L1164): built with just `encode_jbd2_header`; `h_chksum_type`, `h_chksum_size`, and `h_chksum[0]` at offset 16 are all zero bytes. No CRC written.

The replay-side `verify_jbd2_block_checksum` (L282-L321) checks all three types when `sb.has_checksum()` is true:

- Descriptor/revoke: reads `stored = read_be_u32(block, bs-4)`, computes via `checksum_jbd2_tail_zeroed_block(block, seed)`, compares.
- Commit: reads `stored = read_be_u32(block, JBD2_COMMIT_CHKSUM_OFFSET)` (offset 16), computes `!crc32c_append(!seed, &temp)` with the 4 bytes at 16 zeroed, compares.

For writer-produced blocks, `stored == 0` in every case but `computed` is a non-zero CRC of the block bytes, so the comparison returns false and `replay_jbd2_inner` breaks out of the scan (L523).

## Impact

A writer-produced transaction on a checksummed journal (any of `JBD2_FEATURE_COMPAT_CHECKSUM`, `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V2`, `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3`, or `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_ASYNC_COMMIT`) is unreplayable with the default `ReplayOptions { verify_checksums: true }`. Modern `mkfs.ext4` enables `metadata_csum` by default, which sets `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3` on the journal superblock, so any real-world ext4 image with default features falls into this path.

## Why this is currently latent (but still a bug)

`Jbd2Writer::attach_jbd2_writer` is not called from any production binary today (grep across `crates/ffs-fuse`, `crates/ffs-cli`, `crates/ffs-harness`, `crates/ffs` finds no callers); only tests construct writers. Tests avoid triggering the bug by either running without a JBD2 superblock in the journal, or by post-stamping checksums via the `stamp_commit_checksum` / `stamp_descriptor_or_revoke_checksum` helpers (lines 2591-2608), which exist precisely because the writer does not.

When anyone wires `attach_jbd2_writer` into the `ffs mount --rw` / `commit_transaction_journaled` path (see `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:4312`), a checksummed ext4 image will fail to replay on the next mount, silently losing the transaction's writes.

## Suggested fix

1. Add `has_checksum: bool` and `csum_seed: u32` fields to `Jbd2Writer` alongside `is_64bit`.
2. `Jbd2Writer::open` already reads the journal superblock (L889) — have it also populate these fields via `sb.has_checksum()` and `sb.csum_seed()`.
3. Provide a `Jbd2Writer::with_checksum_config(region, start_seq, has_checksum, csum_seed)` constructor for the `Jbd2Writer::new` callers that need it.
4. In `commit_transaction`, when `has_checksum` is true:
   - After filling a descriptor block but before `dev.write_block`, stamp the tail via `checksum_jbd2_tail_zeroed_block(&desc, self.csum_seed).unwrap()` and write into the last 4 bytes.
   - Same for revoke blocks.
   - For commit blocks, zero `h_chksum[0]` (offset 16), compute `!crc32c::crc32c_append(!seed, &commit)`, write into offset 16.
5. Fix `max_tags_per_descriptor` / `max_revoke_entries` / `blocks_needed` to reserve 4 tail bytes when `has_checksum` is true.

## Acceptance

- New regression test: build a `MemBlockDevice`, stamp a valid JBD2 superblock with `feature_incompat = JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3` + a non-zero UUID at region block 0, call `Jbd2Writer::open` (or `with_checksum_config`), commit one transaction with a single write, then call `replay_jbd2_with_options(cx, dev, region, ReplayOptions { verify_checksums: true })` and assert `outcome.stats.replayed_blocks == 1` and the target block was overwritten with the payload.
- Existing non-checksummed tests keep passing.
- `commit_transaction_journaled` in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` — still has no production caller, but when it does, the journal round-trips through a verify_checksums=true replay.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] — real data-loss bug with a clear trigger (metadata_csum ext4 + Jbd2Writer write path), but unreachable from any shipping binary today. Worth fixing before anyone wires the writer into the mount --rw path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:52:19.015585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:58:44.436959096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:58:44.435067924Z","close_reason":"Jbd2Writer stamps descriptor, revoke, and commit checksums for verified replay","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-wse","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement symlink handling (fast/slow) [phased]","description":"Future task: support symlinks.

Deliverables:
- Fast symlinks stored inline in inode i_block.
- Slow symlinks stored as file data.

Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-wu5y3","title":"add mounted-path ext4 and btrfs hard-link-to-symlink type-preservation coverage","description":"Survey finding: mounted-path hard-link coverage currently proves only regular-file behavior, while POSIX requires hard-linking a symlink to create another name for the symlink inode rather than its target. ffs-core has btrfs proof for this contract and ext4 follows the same link path shape, but fuse_e2e does not freeze the operator-visible result. Scope: add ext4 and btrfs mounted-path probes that hard-link an existing symlink, assert the new path remains a symlink via lstat, and verify readlink returns the same target instead of dereferencing to the target inode. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes hard-link-to-symlink public behavior on both filesystems.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:40.301317229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:20:42.691336970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T04:17:34.236386748Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. See commit message bodies of prior fuse_e2e.rs work for per-bead test citations (bd-wu5y3: fuse_hard_link_to_symlink_preserves_symlink_type + btrfs_fuse_*; bd-ynh1e: fuse_rename_over_nonempty_directory_reports_enotempty; bd-p8uaj: btrfs_fuse_rename_over_same_inode_hardlink_is_noop; bd-sex56: btrfs_fuse_hard_link_unlink_updates_nlink_accounting; bd-7xfny: fuse_xattr_ext4_value_too_large_reports_einval + boundary tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-x0lv6","title":"[FUSE] inotify watch lifecycle + IN_MOVED_FROM/TO atomic rename pairing","description":"Wire FUSE notification path so inotify(7) watchers get correct IN_CREATE / IN_DELETE / IN_MOVED_FROM / IN_MOVED_TO events with the kernel-required cookie pairing.

Context:
- ext4/btrfs publish inotify events via fsnotify(); rename(2) atomically issues IN_MOVED_FROM (from old parent) and IN_MOVED_TO (to new parent) sharing the same 32-bit cookie. Watchers rely on the cookie to reconstruct the rename.
- Currently ffs-fuse synthesises directory mutations but does not push fuse_notify_inval_entry / fuse_notify_inval_inode messages to the kernel for the moved subtree, so watchers see DELETE+CREATE instead of MOVE pair.
- Lifecycle: IN_IGNORED on watch removal; IN_Q_OVERFLOW handling when the kernel queue overflows during a burst.
- Reference: fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c, fs/notify/fsnotify.c, FUSE notify protocol opcode FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY (5) + FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_INODE (4) + FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE (6).

Acceptance:
- Test: spawn a thread that runs inotify_init1 + inotify_add_watch on parent dir of a mounted FUSE path, perform N=100 random renames, assert exactly N IN_MOVED_FROM / IN_MOVED_TO pairs with matching cookies (no orphan halves).
- A second test: rename across directories also produces the cross-dir pair, and DELETE+CREATE never appears for plain renames.
- xfstests generic/{348,495,646} (inotify rename) pass under the FUSE mount.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:15.259970709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:15.259970709Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-x0lv6","title":"[FUSE] inotify watch lifecycle + IN_MOVED_FROM/TO atomic rename pairing","description":"Wire FUSE notification path so inotify(7) watchers get correct IN_CREATE / IN_DELETE / IN_MOVED_FROM / IN_MOVED_TO events with the kernel-required cookie pairing.

Context:
- ext4/btrfs publish inotify events via fsnotify(); rename(2) atomically issues IN_MOVED_FROM (from old parent) and IN_MOVED_TO (to new parent) sharing the same 32-bit cookie. Watchers rely on the cookie to reconstruct the rename.
- Currently ffs-fuse synthesises directory mutations but does not push fuse_notify_inval_entry / fuse_notify_inval_inode messages to the kernel for the moved subtree, so watchers see DELETE+CREATE instead of MOVE pair.
- Lifecycle: IN_IGNORED on watch removal; IN_Q_OVERFLOW handling when the kernel queue overflows during a burst.
- Reference: fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c, fs/notify/fsnotify.c, FUSE notify protocol opcode FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY (5) + FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_INODE (4) + FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE (6).

Acceptance:
- Test: spawn a thread that runs inotify_init1 + inotify_add_watch on parent dir of a mounted FUSE path, perform N=100 random renames, assert exactly N IN_MOVED_FROM / IN_MOVED_TO pairs with matching cookies (no orphan halves).
- A second test: rename across directories also produces the cross-dir pair, and DELETE+CREATE never appears for plain renames.
- xfstests generic/{348,495,646} (inotify rename) pass under the FUSE mount.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:15.259970709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:34:31.480658234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:34:31.478775029Z","close_reason":"Completed: FUSE notifier captures kernel notification handle for mount paths, invalidates committed unlink/rmdir/rename dentries without FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE for plain renames, adds mounted inotify rename cookie-pair probes, and updates parity to 96/96; fmt/check/clippy/UBS clean; mounted probes compile/pass via CI soft-skip where FUSE mount permission is denied.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x2io","title":"Add btrfs tree block adversarial fuzz regression coverage","description":"Fallback /testing-fuzzing parity round: add deterministic synthetic adversarial corpus samples and focused regression assertions for btrfs tree block header/item parser boundaries, updating conformance coverage notes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:14:21.520898563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T05:34:25.522549608Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T05:34:25.520289431Z","close_reason":"Added btrfs tree block adversarial fuzz regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fuzz","ondisk","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-x4l3t","title":"[FUSE] vendored fuser protocol upgrade: 7.31 -> 7.40 + FUSE_NOTIFY_* + FUSE_STATX","description":"Prerequisite for bd-bx1wu (memfd seals), bd-4uyu5 (RWF_*/io_uring), bd-x0lv6 (inotify), bd-qpoys (splice/sendfile), and bd-ntb4l-followup (STATX_BTIME on Linux).

Currently vendor/fuser carries abi-7-31 only; on Linux the resulting fuse_attr has no crtime, the FUSE_NOTIFY_* opcodes are not exposed, no FUSE_FCNTL extension exists, and write_flags carries only FUSE_WRITE_CACHE / FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER.

Required:
- Bring vendor/fuser up to abi-7-40 (libfuse 3.x baseline) by porting upstream fuser commits.
- Surface FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY / FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_INODE / FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE so the FrankenFS backend can invalidate the kernel dcache on mutations not initiated through this FUSE mount (needed for inotify event correctness).
- Surface FUSE_STATX (proto 7.35) so STATX_BTIME reaches userspace via a real wire path instead of the proto-7.31 FUSE_GETATTR fallback that drops crtime.
- Carry the io_uring / RWF_* flag bits from FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID / RWF_* so OpenFs::ext4_write can branch on RWF_DSYNC -> immediate fsync, RWF_NOWAIT -> try_lock on FuseInodeLocks, RWF_HIPRI -> nop (no priority queue).
- splice/sendfile fast path needs at minimum FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH advertisement (libfuse 3.16+) for the file types that route directly to the upper layer.

Acceptance: vendor/fuser exposes the new opcodes/flags as Rust types; FrankenFS uses them in the four downstream beads (bd-bx1wu, bd-4uyu5, bd-x0lv6, bd-qpoys); their respective xfstests pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:39:04.698266636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T18:22:18.585554329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-x4o7o","title":"add mounted-path ext4 symlink target PATH_MAX boundary and ENAMETOOLONG coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 symlink creation/readback success is covered on the mounted path, and ffs-core already rejects empty or >PATH_MAX targets with ENAMETOOLONG while accepting the exact PATH_MAX boundary, but fuse_e2e does not freeze that operator-visible contract. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that create a PATH_MAX-sized symlink target successfully, reject an empty target and an over-limit target with ENAMETOOLONG, and verify no stray entries appear after rejection. Acceptance: fuse_e2e proves ext4 symlink target-length boundary behavior on the real mount surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:59:52.449353806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:46.891119870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:46.888876378Z","close_reason":"fuse_symlink_target_path_max_boundary_and_enametoolong_contract at crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs:3167 (commit 92c30f3) freezes ext4 PATH_MAX symlink contract via shared helper assert_symlink_target_path_max_contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
